column1: # POS 2643 = 記号,一般 (Mozc id.def) — same POS used for π, which places
# symbols as candidates after common kanji/katakana conversions.
#   10000  Greek letters — defer to existing katakana loanwords (シグマ, アルファ…)
#           because the reranker penalizes katakana surfaces, and we don't want
#           the Greek glyph to outrank them for everyday input.
#    5000  Math operators, number sets, arrows, geometry — no strong katakana
#           competitors, so mid-cost lets the symbol sit close to the kanji.
# --- Greek letters (uppercase; visually distinct from Latin only) ---
# Cost tiers follow the same convention as above:
#   10000  Readings with common katakana conversions (はーと→ハート, etc.)
# --- Card suits (katakana competitors: スペード, クラブ, ハート, ダイヤ) ---
# --- Check marks (katakana competitor: チェック) ---
